content.full_text:
California
Fair Political
Practices Commission

February 27, 1991


John R. McCarthy, P.E.
Director of Public Works
City of El Paso de Robles
P.O. Box 307
Paso Robles, CA 93447-0307

Re: Your Request for Advice
Our File No. A-91-100

Dear Mr. McCarthy:

This is in response to your letter requesting advice regarding your responsibilities as Director of Public Works and City Engineer for the City of El Paso de Robles under the conflict-of-interest provisions of the Political Reform Act (the "Act").¹

QUESTION

As Director of Public Works and City Engineer for the City of El Paso de Robles may you participate in governmental decisions which may affect your former employer?

CONCLUSION

According to your facts, it does not appear that your prior employment with North Coast Engineering, Inc. will result in a conflict of interest under the Political Reform Act.

FACTS

On January 2, 1991, you were appointed to the position of Public Works Director and City Engineer for the City of El Paso de Robles. As part of your official duties you supervise the city's engineering division. Your job also entails the authority to sign

___________________________
1 Government Code Sections 81000-91015. All statutory references are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated. Commission regulations appear at 2 California Code of Regulations Section 18000, et seq. All references to regulations are to Title 2, Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations.


428 J Street, Suite 800 • P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento CA 95804-0807 • (916)322-5660

Page 2

engineering plans for the city, make recommendations regarding
development proposals, and provide project inspection services.

According to the information provided by you,² for five
years, prior to your appointment, you were employed by North Coast
Engineering, Inc. ("North Coast") which provides civil engineering
services to private clients and to the city. You received income
from North Coast as an employee of North Coast, all of which was
received or accrued prior to the time you became a public
official. You possess no ownership interest in North Coast and do
not intend to return to North Coast as an employee in the future.

North Coast continues to be active before the city and
regularly submits plans to the city engineering division for
approval. You have become concerned that your prior employment
with North Coast will create a conflict of interest with respect
to decisions that may directly and indirectly affect North Coast.

ANALYSIS

Section 87100 prohibits any public official from making,
participating in making, or otherwise using his or her official
position to influence a governmental decision in which the
official has a financial interest. A "public official" is defined
in Regulation 18700 (copy enclosed) to include every natural
person who is a member, officer, employee, or consultant of a
state or local government agency. (Section 82048.) This
definition would include your position as director of public works
and city engineer.

Section 87103 specifies that an official has a financial
interest if it is reasonably foreseeable that the decision will
have a material financial effect, distinguishable from the effect
on the public generally, on the official or a member of his or her
immediate family or on:

(c) Any source of income aggregating two
hundred fifty dollars ($250) or more in value
provided to, received by, or promised to the public
official within 12 months prior to the time when
the decision is made.

Therefore, as a general rule, any person or business that has
made any payment to you in the past 12 months is a source of
income to you for the purposes of Section 87103(c). Thus, North
Coast, as your employer, would be a source of income to you, and

_________________________________________________________________

² Part of this information was set forth in your letter and part
of this information was obtained from you in the course of our
telephone conversation of February 22, 1991.

Page 3


would continue to be a source of income for 12 months after the
last payment North Coast made to you of $250 or more.3

However, Regulation 18704 (copy enclosed) provides a limited
exception which appears to apply to your situation. Pursuant to
Regulation 18704, a former employer is not a source of income
under Section 87103(c) if all of the following apply: (1) All
income from the employer was received by or accrued to the public
official prior to the time he or she became a public official,
and; (2) The income was received in the normal course of the
previous employment; and, (3) There was no expectation by the
public official at the time he or she assumed office of renewed
employment with the former employer. (See Allen Advice Letter,
No. A-85-136; Ruiz Advice Letter; No. A-84-268, copies enclosed.)
If Regulation 18704 applies to your situation, North Coast will
not be considered to be a source of income to you which might lead
to a conflict of interest.

If you have any further questions regarding this matter,
please feel free to contact me at (916) 322-5901.

Sincerely,

Scott Hallabrin
Acting General Counsel

By: John W. Wallace
Counsel, Legal Division

SH:JWW:aa

Enclosures


_______________________

3 Further, Section 82030 provides that the income of an
individual also includes a pro-rata share of any income of any
business entity or trust in which the individual or spouse owns,
directly, indirectly or beneficially, a 10-percent interest or
greater. (Russell Advice Letter, No. A-88-484, copy enclosed.)
However, you stated you have no ownership interest in North Coast.
Therefore, the clients of North Coast are not treated as economic
interests of yours.

4 If you determine that Regulation 18704 does not apply and that
North Coast is a "source of income" to you under the Act, you may
want to contact us for further advice as to whether this would
required you to disqualify yourself from making or participating
in a particular decision.
